vcs","html_url":"https://github.com/jdavcs","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":64,"closed_issues":174,"state":"open","created_at":"2024-05-14T14:03:11Z","updated_at":"2024-10-15T15:49:45Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":1,"created_at":"2024-09-25T09:11:46Z","updated_at":"2024-10-16T13:19:53Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/18887","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/18887","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/18887.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/18887.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"These changes enable editing of extra user preferences (if enabled in Galaxy conf) for users that are authorized remotely (use_remote_user: true). In the current version, when authorized remotely, the extra user preferences interface in the webapp is automatically disabled together with the editing of email address and username. This PR enables the editing of the extra user preferences, while still disabling editing email  and username.

Fixes #18823  

Files changed:
- front-end: UserPreferencesModel.js (to make the 'Manage information' link appear when authorized remotely)
- middleware: remoteuser.py (added the user/information URL to allowed paths)
- API: users.py (to disable email/username when authorized remotely)

## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [x] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. Set `use_remote_user: true` in Galaxy conf and implement remote auth (for instance by injecting REMOTE_USER in your request headers)
  2. Enable extra user preferences via `config/user_preferences_extra_conf.yml` and add some preference input for testing.
  3. Restart Galaxy, and test via User > Preferences > Manage information

## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18823","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18823/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18823/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18823/events","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18823","id":2530819523,"node_id":"I_kwDOAdw-Nc6W2T3D","number":18823,"title":"Extra user preferences not available when using remote users","user":{"login":"dometto","id":147111,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0NzExMQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/147111?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto","html_url":"https://github.com/dometto","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-09-17T10:51:16Z","updated_at":"2024-09-17T10:51:16Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## Description

In order to let users set [vault secrets or extra user preferences](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/master/admin/special_topics/vault.html), which can then be leveraged in tools and data/files sources, the user needs access to the `/users/information` route.  However, this currently is impossible when using the `use_remote_user: true` configuration option. Access to the relevant route is blocked [here](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/remoteuser.py#L163-L202).

I imagine this is because the `/user/information` route also allows the user to edit their email address and username, which is clearly undesirable when these are being provided by an external identity provider. However, at the moment this blocks any Galaxy instance using remote users from using important Galaxy features (vault secrets and extra user preferences).

I've investigated a little bit and was able to verify that in principle, it should be possible to use user preferences/vault with remote users. See below.

Apologies if I've missed a way of setting user preferences and vault secrets when using remote users, and would be very grateful if someone could point me in the right direction if so!

## Suggested fix

I would suggest either:

1. Creating a separate route/view, e.g. `/user/extra_preferences`, in which the fields defined in `user_preferences_extra_conf.yml` can be set.
2. Making the controller and view logic for `/user/information` a bit more flexible, so that it allows setting the extra preferences but not changing email and username, when `use_remote_user` is `true`.

### Verifications

I've tried to test the contours of the above solutions by simply adding `/user/information` to the [allow list](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/remoteuser.py#L163) here. When doing so, I can successfully reference user preferences from inside a tool, with `use_remote_user` set to `true`.

So it indeed seems that the only thing blocking this is the fact that the UI blocks the relevant route.

In order to let users set [vault secrets or extra user preferences](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/master/admin/special_topics/vault.html), which can then be leveraged in tools and data/files sources, the user needs access to the `/users/information` route.  However, this currently is impossible when using the `use_remote_user: true` configuration option. Access to the relevant route is blocked [here](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/remoteuser.py#L163-L202).

I imagine this is because the `/user/information` route also allows the user to edit their email address and username, which is clearly undesirable when these are being provided by an external identity provider. However, at the moment this blocks any Galaxy instance using remote users from using important Galaxy features (vault secrets and extra user preferences).

I've investigated a little bit and was able to verify that in principle, it should be possible to use user preferences/vault with remote users. See below.

Apologies if I've missed a way of setting user preferences and vault secrets when using remote users, and would be very grateful if someone could point me in the right direction if so!

## Suggested fix

I would suggest either:

1. Creating a separate route/view, e.g. `/user/extra_preferences`, in which the fields defined in `user_preferences_extra_conf.yml` can be set.
2. Making the controller and view logic for `/user/information` a bit more flexible, so that it allows setting the extra preferences but not changing email and username, when `use_remote_user` is `true`.

### Verification

I've tried to test the contours of the above solutions by simply adding `/user/information` to the [allow list](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/remoteuser.py#L163) here. rl":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon","html_url":"https://github.com/dannon","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dannon/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false}],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-09-17T10:51:16Z","updated_at":"2024-09-25T09:25:17Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"## Description

In order to let users set [vault secrets or extra user preferences](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/master/admin/special_topics/vault.html), which can then be leveraged in tools and data/files sources, the user needs access to the `/users/information` route.  However, this currently is impossible when using the `use_remote_user: true` configuration option. Access to the relevant route is blocked [here](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/remoteuser.py#L163-L202).

I imagine this is because the `/user/information` route also allows the user to edit their email address and username, which is clearly undesirable when these are being provided by an external identity provider. However, at the moment this blocks any Galaxy instance using remote users from using important Galaxy features (vault secrets and extra user preferences).

I've investigated a little bit and was able to verify that in principle, it should be possible to use user preferences/vault with remote users. See below.

Apologies if I've missed a way of setting user preferences and vault secrets when using remote users, and would be very grateful if someone could point me in the right direction if so!

## Suggested fix

I would suggest either:

1. Creating a separate route/view, e.g. `/user/extra_preferences`, in which the fields defined in `user_preferences_extra_conf.yml` can be set.
2. Making the controller and view logic for `/user/information` a bit more flexible, so that it allows setting the extra preferences but not changing email and username, when `use_remote_user` is `true`.

### Verification

I've tried to test the contours of the above solutions by simply adding `/user/information` to the [allow list](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/blob/dev/lib/galaxy/web/framework/middleware/remoteuser.py#L163) here. When doing so, I can successfully reference user preferences from inside a tool, with `use_remote_user` set to `true`.

So it indeed seems that the only thing blocking this is the fact that the UI blocks the relevant route.

sorry for accidentally having opened this PR and some others against this repo. I was doing some work refactoring this role over at https://github.com/dometto/ansible-galaxy-tools. I've now more or less finished work on it. What I've done includes:

- Making the role compatible with modern ansible versions
- Refactored the scripts used to install tools and workflows
- Simplifying the role somewhat (let the user supply their own restart galaxy handler)
- Adding molecule tests
- Adding linting

This makes the role pretty robust and usable now in my opinion. We are using it to deploy Galaxy servers in the cloud at Utrecht University. I'm wondering if you have any interest in us contributing back the changes to this repo, to ensure maintenance. (I would be willing to act as a co-maintainer for the repo if so.)","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-galaxy-tools/issues/comments/2304821380/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

#207

This PR:

1. Adds optional support for deploying the prebuilt client in `client.yml`.
2. Splits up the installation of node and yarn into a new includes file so that it can be used in both strategies (prebuilt and build).
3. Adds a molecule scenario to test the prebuilt client, refactoring the `pre_tasks` from the default scenario into a separate file.

#207

This PR:

1. Adds optional support for deploying the prebuilt client in `client.yml`.
2. Splits up the installation of node and yarn into a new includes file so that it can be used in both strategies (prebuilt and build).
3. Adds a molecule scenario to test the prebuilt client, refactoring the `pre_tasks` from the default scenario into a separate file.

#207

This PR:

1. Adds optional support for deploying the prebuilt client in `client.yml`.
2. Splits up the installation of node and yarn into a new includes file so that it can be used in both strategies (prebuilt and build).
3. Adds a molecule scenario to test the prebuilt client, refactoring the `pre_tasks` from the default scenario into a separate file.

If not, I would be happy to open a PR. The ability to support the prebuilt client is crucial for us as we are aiming to do cloud deployments of galaxy, and building is slowing down deployment unacceptably long. Optionally using the prebuilt client in ansible would be a great solution!

In fact, I've modified the `client.yml` tasks and already got this to work in its most basic form with:

```yaml
- name: Install prebuilt client via with yarn
  yarn:
    executable: "yarn --network-timeout 300000 --check-files"
    path: "{{ galaxy_server_dir }}"
  environment:
    PATH: "{{ galaxy_venv_dir }}/bin:{{ ansible_env.PATH }}"
    VIRTUAL_ENV: "{{ galaxy_venv_dir }}"
  when: not __galaxy_from_git.stat.exists or (__galaxy_client_build_version != __galaxy_current_commit_id)

- name: Stage prebuilt client
  command: "yarn run stage"
  args:
    chdir: "{{ galaxy_server_dir }}"
  environment:
    PATH: "{{ galaxy_server_dir }}/client/node_modules/.bin:{{ galaxy_venv_dir }}/bin:{{ ansible_env.PATH }}"
    VIRTUAL_ENV: "{{ galaxy_venv_dir }}"
  when: "__galaxy_major_version is version('23.0', '>=')"
```

I noticed that the `client_build_hash.txt` does not match the expect git commit value, so currently the role executes these steps every time I run my playbook, but I assume this would be fixed as soon as a new release of the prebuilt client is done?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-galaxy/issues/comments/2034523964/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

This PR:

1. Adds optional support for deploying the prebuilt client in `client.yml`.
2. Splits up the installation of node and yarn into a new includes file so that it can be used in both strategies (prebuilt and build).
3. Adds a molecule scenario to test the prebuilt client, refactoring the `pre_tasks` from the default scenario into a separate file.

{"action":"opened","number":211,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-galaxy/pulls/211","id":1823441931,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAWidVc5sr4QL","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/ansible-galaxy/pull/211","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/ansible-galaxy/pull/211.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/ansible-galaxy/pull/211.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/ansible-galaxy/issues/211","number":211,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"Update best pracrtice in README.md","user":{"login":"dometto","id":147111,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjE0NzExMQ==","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/147111?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto","html_url":"https://github.com/dometto","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/dometto/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"Hi all,

I tried following the best practices guide in the playbook I'm working on and noticed I had to deviate from it at some points. Maybe I did something wrong, but wanted to open this PR in case the best practice indeed needs to be updated. I'm pretty sure about the following changes:

* update to the postgres_objects role name
* `supervisorctl` should not be used

I needed to add the `systemd` `post_tasks` to actually get Galaxy to start up the first time, but maybe there's a better way for this? all,

I tried following the best practices guide in the README in the playbook I'm working on and noticed I had to deviate from it at some points. Maybe I did something wrong, but wanted to open this PR in case the best practice indeed needs to be updated. I'm pretty sure about the following changes:

* update to the postgres_objects role name
* `supervisorctl` should not be used

I needed to add the `systemd` `post_tasks` to actually get Galaxy to start up the first time, but maybe there's a better way for this? all,

I tried following the best practices guide in the README in the playbook I'm working on and noticed I had to deviate from it at some points. Maybe I did something wrong, but wanted to open this PR in case the best practice indeed needs to be updated. I'm pretty sure about the following changes:

* update to the postgres_objects role name
* `supervisorctl` should not be used

I needed to add the `systemd` `post_tasks` to actually get Galaxy to start up the first time, but maybe there's a better way for this? all,

I tried following the best practices guide in the README in the playbook I'm working on and noticed I had to deviate from it at some points. Maybe I did something wrong, but wanted to open this PR in case the best practice indeed needs to be updated. I'm pretty sure about the following changes:

* update to the postgres_objects role name
* `supervisorctl` should not be used

I needed to add the `systemd` `post_tasks` to actually get Galaxy to start up the first time, but maybe there's a better way for this? all,

I tried following the best practices guide in the README in the playbook I'm working on and noticed I had to deviate from it at some points. Maybe I did something wrong, but wanted to open this PR in case the best practice indeed needs to be updated. I'm pretty sure about the following changes:

* update to the postgres_objects role name
* `supervisorctl` should not be used

I needed to add the `systemd` `post_tasks` to actually get Galaxy to start up the first time, but maybe there's a better way for this? On the failing containers, `/srv/galaxy/var/gravity/supervisor/supervisord.conf.d/_default_.d/galaxy_gunicorn_gunicorn.conf` has:

```
command         = /srv/galaxy/venv/bin/galaxyctl --config-file /srv/galaxy/server/config/galaxy.yml.sample exec _default_ gunicorn
```

It looks like this is because the systemd unit file doesn't correctly set the `GRAVITY_CONFIG_FILE` environment variable.

Additionally, I locally ran into some issues requiring further `apt` dependencies to be installed on Ubuntu in order for some of Galaxy's dependencies to compile correctly. This does not seem to have lead to test failures on CI, my hunch is that this due to me using an `ARM` based machine locally, but I'm not sure. I've added the extra `apt` dependencies that I needed as they seem pretty sane, but feel free to chuck out that commit, of course noticed the reason why the molecule runs for version `23.0` were failing was that `supervisord` was attempting to run Galaxy with the wrong config file. On the failing containers, `/srv/galaxy/var/gravity/supervisor/supervisord.conf.d/_default_.d/galaxy_gunicorn_gunicorn.conf` has:

```
command         = /srv/galaxy/venv/bin/galaxyctl --config-file /srv/galaxy/server/config/galaxy.yml.sample exec _default_ gunicorn
```

It looks like this is because the systemd unit file doesn't correctly set the `GRAVITY_CONFIG_FILE` environment variable.

Additionally, I locally ran into some issues requiring further `apt` dependencies to be installed on Ubuntu in order for some of Galaxy's dependencies to compile correctly. This does not seem to have lead to test failures on CI, my hunch is that this due to me using an `ARM` based machine locally, but I'm not sure. I've added the extra `apt` dependencies that I needed as they seem pretty sane, but feel free to chuck out that commit, of course
